leadText: Extreme Speed (Japanese: しんそく God Speed), formatted as ExtremeSpeed prior to Pokémon X and Y, is a damage-dealing Normal-type move introduced in Generation II. Prior to Pokémon Crystal Version, it was the signature move of Arcanine.

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Contents 1 Effect 1.1 Generation II to IV 1.2 Generation V onwards 1.3 Generation IX 1.3.1 Pokémon Legends: Z-A Effect [ edit | edit source ] Generation II to IV [ edit | edit source ] ExtremeSpeed inflicts damage. It has a priority of +1, so is used before all moves that do not have increased priority. Generation V onwards [ edit | edit source ] Extreme Speed now has +2 priority. Generation IX [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon Legends: Z-A [ edit | edit source ] Like all other moves that previously possessed priority, Extreme Speed causes the user to dash forward to make damaging contact with the target (although terrain may interfere with doing so). During the dash, the user is intangible and will ignore projectiles and entry hazards that they pass through. Pokémon Legends: Z-A Category Physical Accuracy 100% Standard Plus Move Power 65 78 Cooldown 3s Move wind-up time 0 frames Effect duration —s Additional effect — — — None

This attack always goes before any other move. B W B2 W2 The user charges the target at blinding speed. This attack always goes before any other move. sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description MD R B Inflicts damage on a target up to two tiles ahead. MD T D S Inflicts damage on a target up to 2 tiles away. B S L てきポケモンに ダメージをあたえる ２マスまで とどく MD GtI UNUSED Super Smash Bros. for Nintendo 3DS/Wii U A dash through the air that can be steered with (joystick). * S MD MD RT DX It damages an enemy. It reaches up to 2 tiles away. Super Smash Bros. Ultimate Dashes through the air and attacks at the end. Can swerve midflight with directional input. *

sections.title: Trivia
sections.text: Trivia [ edit | edit source ] Extreme Speed's in-game descriptions erroneously state that it goes before any other move. This trait is shared with Baby-Doll Eyes , Bullet Punch , Ice Shard , Mach Punch , Quick Attack , Shadow Sneak , Vacuum Wave , and Water Shuriken . Although it is a Normal-type move, only one Normal-type Pokémon can learn this move by leveling up, not counting Togekiss which was Normal-type prior to Generation VI .
